_comment: Deck source for docs/research/reduced-system-prompt-five-bug-study.md. Source only; no bundled HTML requested.

scenes.title: Reduced System Prompt Five-Bug Study
scenes.subtitle: One clean live result, nine runner-pending cells, no causal A/B yet
scenes.narration: This deck reports what the live run actually produced: one hidden-oracle-scored model result, plus runner failures that prevent a causal prompt comparison today.

scenes.lede: Does the focused replacement prompt cut cost without hurting bug-fix quality?
scenes.body: The causal comparison is narrow: old-append-default at 822f3584 versus focused-replace at e2c2c150, same bugs, same GLM-5.2 profile, same Kitsoki pipeline, same hidden oracles. The prompt path should be the only product variable.
scenes.narration: The research question is unchanged. The comparison must hold the bug, model, treatment, and oracle constant, changing only the prompt path.

scenes.title: Preflight passed before spend

cells: RED at baseline, GREEN at real fix
RED at baseline, GREEN at real fix

scenes.caption: The corpus was armed. The later failures are runner failures, not degenerate fixtures.

scenes.title: What made that cell count

cards.label: No artifact reads
cards.sub: scan found no prior bakeoff paths

scenes.narration: The single valid cell is real evidence. It is live, metered, terminal, hidden-oracle-scored, and the trace did not read prior bakeoff artifacts.

scenes.title: The attempted A/B matrix
scenes.caption: Pending cells failed before trace creation: health class infra:no-trace.

scenes.lede: The live driver did not preserve the no-trace startup error.
scenes.body: After the first solved cell, nine cells failed before session_new wrote a trace. Their drive stderr/stdout logs were empty. Those rows cannot be counted as prompt failures, model failures, or cost data.
scenes.narration: The blocker is in the runner. No trace means no model result, and empty driver logs mean the underlying startup error was lost.

scenes.title: Runner fixes discovered during live spend

cells: ran clean cells in /tmp/kitsoki-rsp-study, copied review artifacts to .artifacts/reduced-system-prompt-five-bug-study/clean-2026-07-06, and scanned traces
still blocking; preserve orchestrator startup error next

scenes.caption: These are harness findings, not evidence for or against the reduced prompt.

scenes.lede: There is still no causal prompt-savings result.
scenes.body: The honest result is narrower: GLM-5.2 solved old-append/query-string/qs1 for $1.3704 across 10 agent calls. The five-bug A/B remains blocked until the remaining nine cells produce trace-backed model results.
scenes.narration: The conclusion is not that focused replacement saves money. The conclusion is that one real cell solved, and the live runner is not reliable enough yet to publish the A/B.

scenes.lede: Fix no-trace observability, then rerun the missing cells.
scenes.body: drive_cell.sh or drive.sh must preserve the orchestrator startup error whenever no session trace is created. Once that is fixed, rerun the nine pending cells under the same /tmp isolation and publish only cells with trace-backed model results.
scenes.narration: The next gate is not another deck. It is a runner fix, then rerunning the missing cells under the same isolation standard.
